title: Verify backward compatibility with existing SpecKV experiments

requirements: Test 3 backward compatibility scenarios: default config (sparse_use_similarity absent), explicit sparse_use_similarity=False, existing scripts without new parameters
Confirm behavior matches original: frequency-only scoring without similarity calculation
Validate no changes to: SparsePruningConfig default behavior, existing YAML configs, existing shell scripts
acceptance: Original script runs without errors: verify by bash run_speckv_aime24_official_sampled8_norm.sh exits with code 0 (dry-run or single question)
Similarity scoring disabled by default: verify self.use_similarity = False when sparse_use_similarity not specified
No similarity calculation occurs: verify cal_similarity not called when self.use_similarity is False (via code inspection or debug output)
Output identical to pre-modification: compare outputs with baseline (if available) or confirm no unexpected behavior changes

context: Similarity scoring guarded by if self.use_similarity check
cal_similarity import does not affect behavior when feature disabled

shared_context.compatibility_requirement: Absolute requirement: no changes to existing experiment behavior
shared_context.test_strategy: Dry-run testing with existing scripts, code path verification, default value validation
conventions: Test with single question for quick validation

artifacts.usage: Reference for backward compatibility requirements and testing checklist
artifacts.contains: Compatibility checklist: run_speckv_aime24_official_sampled8_norm.sh must work identically, sparse_use_similarity=false preserves original behavior

pre_analysis.action: Load context package for artifact paths and smart context
Review existing script to understand expected behavior
Confirm default parameter values in SparsePruningConfig

implementation_approach.title: Create backward compatibility test script
implementation_approach.description: Create test script to validate existing experiments remain unchanged
modification_points: Test scenario 1: SparsePruningConfig with no sparse_use_similarity parameter
Test scenario 3: Load existing YAML config and verify parameters
logic_flow: Import SparsePruningConfig and related classes
Test default initialization: verify use_similarity defaults to False
Test explicit False: verify behavior matches default
Test YAML loading: load sample8_sparseprefillkeep_aime24_official.yaml, confirm no new parameters
Assert no similarity calculation in code path when disabled

implementation_approach.title: Validate existing script dry-run
implementation_approach.description: Run existing script with minimal configuration to verify no errors
modification_points: Execute run_speckv_aime24_official_sampled8_norm.sh with dry-run or single question
Capture exit code and error output
Verify no import errors, no parameter errors, no runtime errors
logic_flow: Modify script temporarily for quick test (single question, reduced sampling)
Execute script and capture output
Check exit code = 0
Check no error messages related to new parameters
Verify script completes without exceptions

implementation_approach.title: Code inspection for conditional logic
implementation_approach.description: Manually inspect code to confirm similarity calculation is properly guarded
modification_points: Review _select_keep_indices method for if self.use_similarity guard
Confirm cal_similarity only called within guarded block
Verify no side effects when similarity disabled
logic_flow: Read _select_keep_indices implementation
Locate if self.use_similarity conditional
Verify cal_similarity and formula only execute when True
Confirm combined variable used unchanged when False
Document code path verification results

implementation_approach.title: Document compatibility verification results
implementation_approach.description: Create verification report confirming backward compatibility
modification_points: Confirm checklist items from design document
Note any issues found and resolutions
logic_flow: Compile test results from all verification steps
Map to compatibility requirements from design document
Document each checklist item: passed/failed/notes
Provide evidence (command outputs, code snippets)
Conclude with overall compatibility status
